displayTitle: Every Flaw Has a Process Address

beats.narration: Before you can read the radiograph, you have to read the weld. Every flaw on film marks a process losing control.

payload.title: READ THE WELD
payload.sub: Every flaw marks a process losing control.

beats.narration: Flux processes trap slag: stick, flux cored, submerged arc. Run cold, MIG leaves lack of fusion. TIG's dipped electrode leaves tungsten in the puddle.

payload.heading: Each process fails its own way

beats.narration: Incomplete penetration stands at the root centerline. Slag rides between passes. Lack of fusion hides along the bevel. Porosity scatters anywhere.

payload.heading: Where each flaw lives in the joint
payload.note: MOST FLAWS RUN PARALLEL TO THE WELD AXIS

beats.narration: Hot cracks split the solidifying centerline. Cold cracks strike the heat affected zone hours to days later. The final radiograph waits 24 to 48 hours.

chips.t: Cold cracks — HAZ, hours to days

beats.narration: So here's the rule: every flaw has a process address. Name the process and the film starts answering questions instead of asking them.

payload.sub: Name the process — the film starts answering.
